endpoint_summary: Retrieves a list of posts. Each post is associated with a user and contains a title and body. The response returns an array of post objects, ordered by their unique identifier.

field_descriptions.userId: The unique identifier of the user who authored the post. This links the post back to a specific user account.
field_descriptions.id: The unique identifier for the post itself. Each post has a distinct numeric ID that can be used to reference or retrieve it individually.
field_descriptions.title: The title or headline of the post, provided as a short summary of the post's content.
field_descriptions.body: The full text content of the post. This is the main body of the message and may contain line breaks.

use_cases: Fetching all posts to display on a blog or feed page
Retrieving posts authored by a specific user by filtering on the userId field
Building a content management dashboard that lists all available posts
Populating a search index with post titles and bodies for full-text search functionality
Displaying a paginated list of posts in a front-end application
example:
GET /posts — Returns an array of post objects. Example item: { "userId": 1, "id": 1, "title": "sunt aut facere repellat provident occaecati excepturi optio reprehenderit", "body": "quia et suscipit
suscipit recusandae consequuntur expedita et cum
reprehenderit molestiae ut ut quas totam
nostrum rerum est autem sunt rem eveniet architecto" }
